title: Unicode Packing Codes

Unicode Packing Codes

Unicode Packing Codes

When Unicode is enabled, Uniface W and C packing codes can contain Unicode characters. The precise mapping and the number of Unicode characters that a field can contain depend on whether the DB2 database was created to use Unicode (UTF-8 encoding) or not (a code page).

Unicode Database Mappings

For a Unicode database

(and also all databases on iSeries) , and depending on the length of the field:

  • W packing code maps to Graphic, Vargraphic, or Long Vargraphic
  • C packing code maps to Char, Varchar, or Long Varchar
  • SW packing codes map to DBCLOB
  • SC packing codes map to CLOB

A W10 field can hold 10 BMP Unicode characters, but only 5 Unicode characters that are outside the BMP range (>0xffff), because those characters are represented by surrogate pairs, that is two W characters per character that is outside the BMP range.

On Windows and Unix, a C10 field can hold 10 ASCII characters, but only 5 characters from the Latin-1 range that are not ASCII, such as accented letters, which in UTF-8 occupy 2 bytes. Simplified Chinese and characters from other languages within BMP occupy 3 bytes, so a C10 field will hold only three such characters.

On iSeries, this applies only when $DEF_CHARSET is set to UTF8 .

Non-Unicode Database Mappings

For a non-Unicode database:

  • W packing code maps to Char, Varchar, or Long Varchar, with CCSID UNICODE added to the table definition.
  • C packing code maps to Char, Varchar, or Long Varchar.

    If $DEF_CHARSET is UTF8, CCSID UNICODE is added to the table definition

  • SW packing codes map to CLOB
  • SC packing codes map to CLOB

A W10 becomes a CHAR(40) in the database; this way any 10 Unicode characters will fit. Although you can use SQL to insert 40 ASCII characters into a W10 field, this will not work in Uniface—it will be chopped to 10 characters when you retrieve such an occurrence.

On DB2 versions lower than 9.7, the contents of CLOB fields (packing code SW and SC) is limited to the database code page—every character that is not part of the database code page is replaced by a substitution character by DB2. This restriction does not apply on DB2 9.7 and higher.

Related Links
  • Support for Unicode in DB2 Connector

    The Uniface DB2 connector enables Unicode support (with some limitations). By default, Unicode support is enabled.

  • Packing Codes for Unicode

    By default, the W packing code is for Unicode characters. The U* packing code can also be used for holding Unicode characters. If the entity character set is Unicode, the C packing code can also be used for Unicode characters.
